Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Stripe for all rate lock extension charges using stripe_search_charges or stripe_list_charges with a query related to "rate lock extension" (or similar) to discover every loan that has been charged an extension fee. The agent should find extension charges across dozens of loans spanning LN-2024-*, LN-2025-*, and LN-2026-* series. Key active-pipeline loans with extension charges include: LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) with 3 charges totaling $1,250 ($375 + $375 + $500), LN-2026-00619 (Rivera) with a succeeded $375 charge plus a failed $450 charge followed by a succeeded $450 retry totaling $825 paid, LN-2026-00521 (Esposito) at $375, LN-2026-00522 (Pham) at $350, LN-2026-00529 (Lombardi) at $425, LN-2026-00555 (Flores) at $400, LN-2026-00557 (Freeman) at $425, and LN-2026-00558 (Reyes) with $400 + $450 totaling $850. Among older loans, three charges appear anomalous at roughly 10x the typical extension fee range of $350–$500 and likely represent data entry errors: LN-2024-00115 (Quinn) at $4,250 (amount field: 425000 cents), LN-2025-00358 (Singh) at $4,000 (amount field: 400000 cents), and LN-2025-00367 (Hall) at $4,750 (amount field: 475000 cents on the 2nd extension charge).
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for rate lock extension-related invoices and bills using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills with a query related to "lock extension" or "extension fee" (or similar) to find corresponding billing records. The agent should discover that QuickBooks invoices contain NO rate lock extension fee entriesonly broker compensation and processing fees. QuickBooks bills contain 3 entries related to extensions, all for the Mercer loan (LN-2026-00184): BILL-2026-0319-001 (DocNumber "LOCK-EXT-00184") for $375 with empty line items and an unpaid balance of $375, BILL-2026-0402-001 for $375 ("1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay," balance: $0, paid), and BILL-2026-0416-002 for $375 ("Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan," balance: $375, still unpaid). Only the latter two bills have explicit extension descriptions in their line items; BILL-2026-0319-001 is identifiable as extension-related only by its doc number. No other loan has any QuickBooks extension fee record.
OE 3: For each loan with a Stripe extension charge, look up the loan in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (by loan number) or mortgage_los_search_loans to check whether the extension is documented in the loan filespecifically the rate_lock_date, rate_lock_expiration, notes, and loan status. For LN-2026-00184 (Mercer), the agent should discover: status is "processing," rate_lock_date is "2026-03-01," rate_lock_expiration is "2026-03-23" (which is already over a month past as of today April 28, 2026, yet three extensions have been charged), notes say "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions," and the rate_lock_expiration has NOT been updated to reflect the extensions. For LN-2026-00619 (Rivera borrower), the agent should discover the loan is at "clear_to_close" status with rate_lock_expiration "2026-03-21" (also past), and the assigned LO is Brian Mitchell (los_staff_6d606f7506a7) who is terminated (is_active: false, termination_date: 2025-04-15).
OE 4: Check the loan activity log for key mismatched loans using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (by loan_id) to determine whether extensions were documented with activity notes in the LOS. The agent should discover that there are NO activity log entries referencing lock extensions for any of the flagged loansthe extensions were charged in Stripe but never logged in the LOS activity feed.
OE 5: Search emails and Slack for context on the extension charges and any existing discussions about the Stripe/QuickBooks discrepancy. Using search_emails with a query related to "lock extension" or "Mercer" or "extension fee" (or similar), and conversations_search_messages with a query related to "lock extension" or "extension" or "Mercer" (or similar) on channels including #rate-watch (C005) and #loan-processing (C002). The agent should find Slack messages in C002 and C005 discussing the Mercer extensions: the 1st extension fee logged ($375) in C002 by Hana Kim, the builder delay pushing completion to 6/12 in C002 by Carlos Rivera, Lauren at Fairview refusing to cover a 2nd extension in C002, Evan Mercer pushing back on paying the 2nd extension in C002, a 2nd extension request note posted in C005 (#rate-watch) by Camille Foster, and Hana setting up a pending $375 hold in QB for the 2nd extension with ownership still unresolved in C002. No Slack or email messages reference a 3rd extension on the Mercer loan, yet Stripe has a 3rd charge for $500.
OE 6: Look up contacts for Grace Yamamoto and Hana Kim using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts to confirm their email addresses: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (Director of Operations) and hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com (Bookkeeper / Accountant). Also look up the email addresses of the loan officers who need to be notified, including Carlos Rivera (carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com), Derek Moss (derek.moss@keystonemortgage.com), Keisha Williams (keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com), Natasha Okafor (natasha.okafor@keystonemortgage.com), and Amy Chen (amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com).
OE 7: Search CRM for borrower contacts related to the mismatched loans using crm_search_contacts with a query related to "Mercer" or "Evan" (or similar) to find the CRM contact record for Evan Mercer (crm_crmcontact_5744dda7fddf) and any existing engagement notes. The agent should discover existing CRM engagement notes for the 1st extension ("1st lock ext paid, $375") and the 2nd extension ("2nd ext needed. Builder delay and first ext already used") but NO engagement note for the 3rd extension. Also search for CRM records related to other borrowers on mismatched loans to identify CRM contact IDs needed for creating engagement notes.
OE 8: For each loan with a confirmed Stripe vs QuickBooks mismatch, add an activity note to the loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity. At minimum, the agent must add an activity note to LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) with a detail describing the mismatch: 3 Stripe extension charges totaling $1,250 versus 3 QuickBooks bills totaling $1,125 (BILL-2026-0319-001 for $375 with empty line items, BILL-2026-0402-001 for $375 paid, and BILL-2026-0416-002 for $375 still unpaid), with the 3rd Stripe extension charge of $500 having no clearly corresponding QuickBooks bill and the rate_lock_expiration not updated in the loan file. Activity notes should also be added to other active loans with unreconciled Stripe charges (LN-2026-00619, LN-2026-00521, LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00529, LN-2026-00555, LN-2026-00557, LN-2026-00558) describing the extension charge amounts found in Stripe with no corresponding QuickBooks records.
OE 9: For each borrower on a mismatched loan, add a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement documenting the billing discrepancy. At minimum, the agent must create an engagement note on Evan Mercer's CRM contact (crm_crmcontact_5744dda7fddf) noting the unreconciled 3rd extension charge of $500 and the need for QuickBooks reconciliation. Engagement notes should also be created for borrowers on the other active mismatched loans.
OE 10: Send an email (via send_email) to each affected loan officer as a direct heads-up about the extension fee discrepancies on their loans. At minimum: an email to carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com regarding LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) detailing the 3rd extension charge ($500) not booked in QuickBooks and the stale rate_lock_expiration, and regarding LN-2026-00558 (Reyes) with $850 in Stripe charges and no QB records. Additional emails should be sent to the other LOs with affected loans: derek.moss@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00557 Freeman), keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00522 Pham, LN-2026-00555 Flores), natasha.okafor@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00521 Esposito), and amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com (LN-2026-00529 Lombardi). The sender should be camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com.
OE 11: Send an email (via send_email) to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com from camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com with the total dollar exposure for her risk review. The email body must include: (a) the total dollar amount of unreconciled Stripe extension charges across all affected loans, (b) specific identification of the Mercer loan (LN-2026-00184) as having the most significant mismatch with a 3rd extension charge of $500 not in QuickBooks, (c) the anomalous 10x charges on older loansQuinn (LN-2024-00115) at $4,250, Singh (LN-2025-00358) at $4,000, and Hall (LN-2025-00367) at $4,750as potential data entry errors, and (d) the finding that LN-2026-00619 (Rivera borrower) is assigned to terminated LO Brian Mitchell.
OE 12: Send an email (via send_email) to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com from camille.foster@keystonemortgage.com about the billing corrections needed. The email body must include: (a) identification of the Mercer loan's 3rd extension ($500 Stripe charge) needing a new QuickBooks bill entry, (b) the Mercer 2nd extension bill (PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER) that still has a $375 unpaid balance with unresolved cost ownership between the borrower and Fairview Homes, (c) the anomalous 10x charges on older loans needing investigation and possible correctionQuinn (LN-2024-00115) at $4,250, Singh (LN-2025-00358) at $4,000, and Hall (LN-2025-00367) at $4,750, and (d) a summary of all other active loans with Stripe extension charges that have no corresponding QuickBooks records.
OE 13: Post the full audit summary in #rate-watch (Slack channel C005) using conversations_add_message. The message should include: (a) the number of loans with Stripe extension charges that have no matching QuickBooks records, (b) the total unreconciled dollar exposure, (c) specific callout of the Mercer loan's 3rd extension gap and the anomalous 10x charges on Quinn ($4,250), Singh ($4,000), and Hall ($4,750), (d) confirmation that activity notes, CRM notes, and LO notifications have been sent, and (e) that Grace and Hana have been briefed on the risk exposure and billing corrections respectively.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Stripe for all rate lock extension charges using stripe_search_charges or stripe_list_charges with a query related to "rate lock extension" (or similar) to discover every loan that has been charged an extension fee. The agent should find extension charges across dozens of loans spanning LN-2024-*, LN-2025-*, and LN-2026-* series. Key active-pipeline loans with extension charges include: LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) with 3 charges totaling $1,250 ($375 + $375 + $500), LN-2026-00619 (Rivera) with a succeeded $375 charge plus a failed $450 charge followed by a succeeded $450 retry totaling $825 paid, LN-2026-00521 (Esposito) at $375, LN-2026-00522 (Pham) at $350, LN-2026-00529 (Lombardi) at $425, LN-2026-00555 (Flores) at $400, LN-2026-00557 (Freeman) at $425, and LN-2026-00558 (Reyes) with $400 + $450 totaling $850. Among older loans, three charges appear anomalous at roughly 10x the typical extension fee range of $350–$500 and likely represent data entry errors: LN-2024-00115 (Quinn) at $4,250 (amount field: 425000 cents), LN-2025-00358 (Singh) at $4,000 (amount field: 400000 cents), and LN-2025-00367 (Hall) at $4,750 (amount field: 475000 cents on the 2nd extension charge).
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for rate lock extension-related invoices and bills using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills with a query related to "lock extension" or "extension fee" (or similar) to find corresponding billing records. The agent should discover that QuickBooks invoices contain NO rate lock extension fee entriesonly broker compensation and processing fees. QuickBooks bills contain 3 entries related to extensions, all for the Mercer loan (LN-2026-00184): BILL-2026-0319-001 (DocNumber "LOCK-EXT-00184") for $375 with empty line items and an unpaid balance of $375, BILL-2026-0402-001 for $375 ("1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay," balance: $0, paid), and BILL-2026-0416-002 for $375 ("Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan," balance: $375, still unpaid). Only the latter two bills have explicit extension descriptions in their line items; BILL-2026-0319-001 is identifiable as extension-related only by its doc number. No other loan has any QuickBooks extension fee record.
OE 3: For each loan with a Stripe extension charge, look up the loan in the Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan (by loan number) or mortgage_los_search_loans to check whether the extension is documented in the loan filespecifically the rate_lock_date, rate_lock_expiration, notes, and loan status. OE 4: Check the loan activity log for key mismatched loans using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (by loan_id) to determine whether extensions were documented with activity notes in the LOS. The agent should discover that there are NO activity log entries referencing lock extensions for any of the flagged loansthe extensions were charged in Stripe but never logged in the LOS activity feed.
OE 5: Search emails and Slack for context on the extension charges and any existing discussions about the Stripe/QuickBooks discrepancy. Using search_emails with a query related to "lock extension" or "Mercer" or "extension fee" (or similar), and conversations_search_messages with a query related to "lock extension" or "extension" or "Mercer" (or similar) on channels including #rate-watch (C005) and #loan-processing (C002). No Slack or email messages reference a 3rd extension on the Mercer loan, yet Stripe has a 3rd charge for $500.
OE 6: Look up contacts for Grace Yamamoto and Hana Kim using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts to confirm their email addresses: grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (Director of Operations) and hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com (Bookkeeper / Accountant). Also look up the email addresses of the loan officers who need to be notified, including Carlos Rivera (carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com), Derek Moss (derek.moss@keystonemortgage.com), Keisha Williams (keisha.williams@keystonemortgage.com), Natasha Okafor (natasha.okafor@keystonemortgage.com), and Amy Chen (amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com).

Activity notes should also be added to other active loans with unreconciled Stripe charges (LN-2026-00619, LN-2026-00521, LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00529, LN-2026-00555, LN-2026-00557, LN-2026-00558) describing the extension charge amounts found in Stripe with no corresponding QuickBooks records.

The email body must include: (a) identification of the Mercer loan's 3rd extension ($500 Stripe charge) needing a new QuickBooks bill entry, (b) the Mercer 2nd extension bill (PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER) that still has a $375 unpaid balance with unresolved cost ownership between the borrower and Fairview Homes, (c) the anomalous 10x charges on older loans needing investigation and possible correctionQuinn (LN-2024-00115) at $4,250, Singh (LN-2025-00358) at $4,000, and Hall (LN-2025-00367) at $4,750, and (d) a summary of all other active loans with Stripe extension charges that have no corresponding QuickBooks records.
OE 13: Post the full audit summary in #rate-watch (Slack channel C005) using conversations_add_message. The message should include: (a) the number of loans with Stripe extension charges that have no matching QuickBooks records, (b) the total unreconciled dollar exposure, (c) specific callout of the Mercer loan's 3rd extension gap and the anomalous 10x charges on Quinn ($4,250), Singh ($4,000), and Hall ($4,750), (d) confirmation that activity notes, CRM notes, and LO notifications have been sent, and (e) that Grace and Hana have been briefed on the risk exposure and billing corrections respectively.
📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a Stripe read tool (e.g., stripe_search_charges, stripe_list_charges, or stripe_get_charge) to discover rate lock extension charges.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used stripe_search_charges with query 'Rate lock extension' (plus two follow-up calls with 'lock extension LN-2025' and 'lock extension LN-2026'). Tool was called and returned results.
✓Run #2The agent called both stripe_list_charges (limit=50) and stripe_search_charges (query='Rate lock extension', limit=50) as well as additional stripe_search_charges calls for 'Mercer', 'Pham', and 'LN-2026'. Stripe read tools were used.
✓Run #3The agent called both stripe_list_charges (limit: 50) and stripe_search_charges (query: 'Rate lock extension', limit: 50) in the trajectory.
✓Run #4The agent called stripe_search_charges (query: 'Rate lock extension') and stripe_list_charges (limit 50), directly querying Stripe for extension charges.
✓Run #5The agent called stripe_list_charges (entry 4) and stripe_search_charges with query 'extension' (entry 14) and 'Rate lock extension' (entry 26). Multiple Stripe read tools were used.
✓Run #6The agent called stripe_list_charges (item 4) and stripe_search_charges multiple times (items 14, 28, 32), directly querying Stripe for extension charges.
2 The model must use a QuickBooks read tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_invoices) to look up extension-related billing records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_bills (with fetchAll:true) and quickbooks_search_invoices (with empty criteria {}). Both are legitimate QB read tool calls used to retrieve extension-related billing records.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll=true) and quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria=null). QuickBooks read tools were used to pull billing records.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_bills twice: once with fetchAll:true for all bills, and once with criteria 'Line.Description LIKE "%extension%"' to filter for extension-related records.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_bills (fetchAll: true) and quickbooks_search_invoices, retrieving QuickBooks billing records for reconciliation.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll:true (entry 6), which is a QuickBooks read tool call. The tool returned no results.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills (item 6) and quickbooks_search_invoices (item 8) to look up extension-related billing records.
3 The model must use a Mortgage LOS read tool (e.g., mortgage_los_get_loan, mortgage_los_search_loans, or mortgage_los_get_pipeline) to look up loan file details for loans with extension charges.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used mortgage_los_get_pipeline, mortgage_los_search_loans, and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (13 calls) to look up loan file details for loans with extension charges.
✓Run #2The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline (limit=50) and mortgage_los_search_loans multiple times. LOS read tools were used.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline to pull all loans and then called mortgage_los_search_loans approximately 14 times to look up individual loan files by ID or borrower name.
✓Run #4The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline (twice), mortgage_los_search_loans (query: 'Mercer'), and mortgage_los_get_loan (three calls) to look up loan file details.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline (entry 2) and then multiple mortgage_los_search_loans calls (entries 28-55) for individual loan lookups.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline (item 2) and mortgage_los_search_loans multiple times (items 26, 42–49, 56) to look up loan file details.
4 The model must use a CRM read tool (e.g., crm_search_contacts or crm_get_contact) to look up borrower contact records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used crm_search_contacts 13 times to look up borrower records by name (Ibrahim, Thompson, Kumar, Zhang, Reynolds, Lombardi, Reyes, Lee, Desai, Graham, Stein, Morales, Davis) before creating CRM engagement notes.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_search_contacts with full_name='Evan Mercer', successfully finding the borrower's CRM contact record (crm_crmcontact_5744dda7fddf).
✓Run #3The agent called crm_search_contacts 14 times to look up borrower contact records for all affected borrowers.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_search_contacts three times (queries: 'Evan Mercer', 'Sandra Kang', 'Angela Foster') to look up borrower CRM contact records.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_search_contacts for 13 borrowers (entries 112-137), finding records for Reginald Ibrahim and Heather Lee.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_contacts multiple times (items 58, 60, 88–95) to look up borrower contact records in the CRM.
5 The model must use mortgage_los_get_activity_log to check existing loan activity entries for loans with extension charges.
tool selection
 ✓✓✗✓✗✓ 67%
✓Run #1The agent used mortgage_los_get_activity_log 5 times on loan IDs (los_loan_3dd27123d2f1, los_loan_4cfc7e85c107, los_loan_3e80605c54da, los_loan_ea01fd4e54c8, los_loan_d03d4973f85c) to check existing activity entries.
✓Run #2The agent called mortgage_los_get_activity_log for four different loan IDs (los_loan_e3c96605df9f, los_loan_946659b8fc80, los_loan_e0934521fda4, los_loan_3662f4616df5, los_loan_b58b585a78ec). Activity log reads were performed for multiple loans with extension charges.
✗Run #3No mortgage_los_get_activity_log call appears anywhere in the 92-tool-call trajectory. The agent used mortgage_los_add_activity, mortgage_los_get_pipeline, and mortgage_los_search_loans, but never checked existing activity logs.
✓Run #4The agent called mortgage_los_get_activity_log for loan los_loan_e3c96605df9f (LN-2026-00184/Mercer) and los_loan_253f2c5cfbe2 (LN-2026-00617), confirming no existing activity log entries.
✗Run #5No mortgage_los_get_activity_log call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent checked loan files via mortgage_los_search_loans but never retrieved the activity log for any loan.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_get_activity_log on the Mercer loan (los_loan_e3c96605df9f) at item 34 and discovered zero activity entries, which became a key finding.
6 The model must pass a query related to "rate lock extension" or "lock extension" or "extension" (or similar) when calling stripe_search_charges.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The first stripe_search_charges call used query 'Rate lock extension', and subsequent calls used 'lock extension LN-2025' and 'lock extension LN-2026' — all directly related to rate lock extensions.
✓Run #2The agent called stripe_search_charges with query='Rate lock extension', which directly matches the criterion requiring an extension-related query term.
✓Run #3The agent called stripe_search_charges with query: 'Rate lock extension', which is directly related to the required search term.
✓Run #4The agent called stripe_search_charges with query 'Rate lock extension', which is directly related to the extension fee search requirement.
✓Run #5stripe_search_charges was called with query 'extension' (entry 14) and then 'Rate lock extension' (entry 26), both clearly extension-related terms.
✓Run #6At item 14, the agent called stripe_search_charges with query 'Rate lock extension', directly matching the criterion's requirement for an extension-related search term.
7 The model must pass a query related to \"lock extension\" or \"extension fee\" or \"rate lock extension\" (or similar) when calling quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_invoices.
query construction
 ✗✗✓✗✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The quickbooks_search_bills call used fetchAll:true (no extension-specific query), and quickbooks_search_invoices used empty criteria {}. Neither passed a query related to 'lock extension,' 'extension fee,' or 'rate lock extension' as required.
✗Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll=true and no query parameter related to 'extension' or 'lock extension'. The agent then used Bash scripts to filter results locally. The QB tool call itself did not pass an extension-related query as required by this criterion.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with criteria 'Line.Description LIKE "%extension%"', which is an appropriate extension-related query.
✗Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll: true and no extension-related query string. The criteria requires a query parameter containing terms like 'lock extension', 'extension fee', or similar.
✗Run #5The quickbooks_search_bills call used fetchAll:true with no extension-related query parameter. The filtering for extension terms was done via a separate Bash tool call after fetching all bills, not via a targeted QuickBooks query.
✗Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_bills with only fetchAll: true (item 6), not with any extension-related query term. The subsequent filtering was done via a Bash command, not via the QuickBooks search tool's query parameter.

outcome

32 The loan activity note on LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) mentions the mismatch between Stripe extension charges (3 charges totaling approximately $1,250) and QuickBooks records, with the 3rd Stripe charge of $500 having no corresponding QuickBooks bill entry.
outcome
 ✗✓✗✗✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1The agent never added an activity to LN-2026-00184 (Mercer), so no activity note exists to check for the $1,250 Stripe vs QuickBooks mismatch detail. The agent's activities targeted LN-2025 loans.
✓Run #2The compliance_alert detail on LN-2026-00184 explicitly states 'STRIPE: 3 extension charges collected totaling $1,250.00' listing each charge, 'QUICKBOOKS: 2 bills totaling $750.00', and '3rd Stripe extension charge of $500.00 has NO corresponding QuickBooks bill.' The mismatch is clearly documented.
✗Run #3The activity note on the Mercer loan (los_loan_e3c96605df9f) references two QB bills totaling $750 with NO Stripe charges. There is no mention of a 3rd Stripe charge of $500 or any Stripe charges at all — the agent found the reverse situation (QB exists, Stripe absent).
✗Run #4The activity note on LN-2026-00184 describes 'Two QB extension bills ($375+$375=$750) with $0 collected in Stripe' — the inverse of what the data shows. The criteria requires the note to reference 3 Stripe charges totaling ~$1,250 with the 3rd $500 charge having no QB match. The agent inverted the reconciliation direction.
✗Run #5No activity note was posted to LN-2026-00184 (Mercer). The agent worked only with 2025-series loans. No Mercer-specific mismatch details (3 charges totaling $1,250, unmatched $500 3rd charge) were documented in any LOS activity note.
✓Run #6The Mercer compliance_alert activity note (items 82–83) mentions '3 Stripe extensions ($1,250) vs 2 QB bills ($750), lock expired,' which captures the 3-charge vs 2-bill mismatch. The $500 gap for the unmatched 3rd charge is implied by the $1,250 Stripe vs $750 QB comparison with 3 vs 2 entries.

35 The email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (visible in send_email parameters) includes an approximate total dollar figure for the unreconciled Stripe extension charges across affected active-pipeline loans (approximately $3,650 to $4,900, depending on reconciliation methodology).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The grace email reports $24,725 total unreconciled exposure and $6,400 for 13 active (2025-vintage) pipeline loans. Neither figure falls in the expected range of $3,650–$4,900 for the correct set of 8 affected 2026-vintage loans.
✗Run #2The email to grace.yamamoto states 'TOTAL UNRECONCILED EXPOSURE: $500.00', far below the expected range of $3,650–$4,900. The agent missed the 7 other active-pipeline loans with unreconciled Stripe extension charges.
✗Run #3The email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com states total exposure of $7,550, which is outside the expected range of $3,650–$4,900. The agent's dataset and reconciliation methodology differ significantly from what the criteria expect.
✗Run #4The email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com mentions $750 uncollected for Mercer and ~$20,700 systematic gap but does not compute or state a total of $3,650–$4,900 for unreconciled active-pipeline loans specifically. The agent conflated the full historical gap with the active-pipeline exposure.
✗Run #5The Grace email cited $24,325 as the total exposure (all 50 Stripe charges across all 42 loans), not the narrower $3,650-$4,900 figure representing only unreconciled active-pipeline loan charges. The email does not include a figure in the expected range.
✗Run #6The Grace email states $24,425 total unreconciled gap, not the active-pipeline-specific figure of $3,650–$4,900. The agent summed all charges including closed/denied loans rather than isolating active-pipeline loans.

✓Run #6The Grace email (items 122–123) mentions Quinn with a '$3,825 probable overcharge' on a charge of $4,250, identifying it as an anomalous amount consistent with a data entry error.

outcome

✓Run #2The email to hana.kim explicitly states 'The 3rd extension charge of $500.00 (Stripe charge ch_scenario_c66_lockext3) was never entered as a bill in QuickBooks' and 'needs a new QB bill created to match Stripe charge ch_scenario_c66_lockext3'.
✗Run #3The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com does not mention a 3rd Stripe extension charge of $500 for Mercer needing a QB bill. The agent found the opposite: Mercer has QB bills but no Stripe charges collected.
✗Run #4The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com describes the Mercer issue as QB bills with no Stripe collection (inverse of the actual finding). It does not identify a 3rd Stripe extension charge of $500 lacking a QB bill entry.
✗Run #5The Hana email is described as instructions to book 50 QB bills matching Stripe charges plus a hold on the $4,250 Quinn charge. No specific reference to Mercer's 3rd extension charge of $500 needing a new QB bill appears.
✓Run #6The Hana email (items 124–125) covers itemized QB corrections for all 6 flagged loans, and the Mercer loan entry references the 3 Stripe charges vs 2 QB bills, implying the 3rd extension charge of ~$500 needs a new QB bill. The trajectory summary explicitly notes 'Mercer' as one of the 6 corrective items.
41 The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com (visible in send_email parameters) identifies the anomalous charge on LN-2024-00115 (Quinn) at approximately $4,250 as needing investigation and possible correction.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The hana email explicitly states: 'LN-2024-00115 (Quinn): $4,250 Stripe charge — roughly 10x typical range. Possible data entry error requiring investigation and potential partial refund.' This matches the criterion.
✗Run #2The email to hana.kim does not mention Quinn (LN-2024-00115) or any anomalous ~$4,250 charge needing investigation.
✗Run #3The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com does not mention Quinn (LN-2024-00115) or any charge of approximately $4,250. No anomalous large charges appear anywhere in the trajectory.
✓Run #4The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com explicitly mentions 'Quinn $4,250 potential over-collection' and asks Hana to 'investigate Quinn $4,250 charge for potential refund', clearly identifying this anomalous charge as needing investigation.
✓Run #5The Hana email (entry 188) explicitly mentions the $4,250 Quinn anomaly with a note to 'hold pending guidance from Grace,' satisfying the requirement to flag Quinn (LN-2024-00115) as needing investigation.
✓Run #6The Hana email covers itemized QB corrections for all 6 flagged loans, and Quinn (LN-2024-00115) is one of those 6, with the probable $3,825 overcharge on a $4,250 charge flagged for investigation and correction.

44 The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com (visible in send_email parameters) identifies the Mercer loan's 2nd extension bill (PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER / BILL-2026-0416-002) as having a $375 unpaid balance with cost ownership unresolved between the borrower (Evan Mercer) and the builder (Fairview Homes).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1The hana email references 'PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER: $375 (unpaid)' for the Mercer loan, noting these QB bills may need to be reversed or matched. This identifies the unpaid $375 balance for the 2nd extension bill with unresolved ownership.
✓Run #2The email to hana.kim explicitly references 'BILL-2026-0416-002: $375.00 — 2nd lock extension (pending, Doc# PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER)' and notes 'The borrower has disputed the 2nd extension fee in writing. Before posting anything further, you may want to confirm with Carlos Rivera on cost ownership resolution.' This covers the pending/unpaid balance and the unresolved cost ownership.
✓Run #3The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com explicitly references 'PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER: $375.00 balance still unpaid' and states the need to reconcile whether the borrower was charged through a different channel, identifying the outstanding cost ownership issue.
✓Run #4The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com references the $375 pending/disputed Mercer bill (PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER) and asks Hana to 'hold the $375 pending bill', with mention of disputed cost ownership between Mercer and builder.
✗Run #5The Hana email does not reference the Mercer 2nd extension bill's unpaid balance ($375) or unresolved cost ownership between Mercer and Fairview Homes. The agent found 0 QB bills in its reconciliation so this nuance was not captured.
✓Run #6The agent found BILL-2026-0416-002 with a $375 unpaid balance and the description about cost ownership pending for the Mercer loan. The Hana email's itemized corrections for the Mercer loan would include this pending bill detail, as the agent explicitly identified this bill in the QB search results (items 16–17).
45 The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com (visible in send_email parameters) includes a summary identifying that all other active-pipeline loans with Stripe extension charges (Rivera/LN-2026-00619, Esposito/LN-2026-00521, Pham/LN-2026-00522, Lombardi/LN-2026-00529, Flores/LN-2026-00555, Freeman/LN-2026-00557, Reyes/LN-2026-00558) have no corresponding QuickBooks bill entries and need new records created.

✗Run #2The email to hana.kim only discusses the Mercer loan (LN-2026-00184). It does not mention Rivera, Esposito, Pham, Lombardi, Flores, Freeman, or Reyes loans needing new QB bill entries. The agent concluded those loans had no Stripe extension charges, missing all seven other affected loans.
✓Run #3The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com lists 13 active-pipeline loans with Stripe extension charges that have no corresponding QuickBooks bill entries and need new records created, with all Stripe charge IDs included.
✗Run #4The email to hana.kim@keystonemortgage.com mentions 50 Stripe charges with no QB entries broadly, but does not specifically identify the active-pipeline loans (Rivera/LN-2026-00619, Esposito/LN-2026-00521, Pham/LN-2026-00522, Lombardi/LN-2026-00529, Flores/LN-2026-00555, Freeman/LN-2026-00557, Reyes/LN-2026-00558) as needing new QB bill entries.
✗Run #5The Hana email broadly says 50 QB bills need to be created but doesn't specifically identify the 7 active-pipeline 2026 loans (Rivera, Esposito, Pham, Lombardi, Flores, Freeman, Reyes). The agent worked with 2025-series loans, not these 2026 loans.
✗Run #6The agent never identified the active-pipeline loans Rivera, Esposito, Pham, Lombardi, Flores, Freeman, or the correct Reyes (LN-2026-00558). The Hana email covers only the 6 incorrect loans the agent identified, not the full set of active-pipeline loans with missing QB entries.
46 The email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com identifies that QuickBooks contains no rate lock extension fee records for any active-pipeline loan except LN-2026-00184 (Mercer); the remaining active-pipeline loans with Stripe extension charges have no corresponding QuickBooks entries.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✗✗ 33%
✓Run #1The grace email states that 50 Stripe charges across 35 loans have 'ZERO matching QuickBooks entries,' while separately identifying QB bills only for Mercer (LN-2026-00184). This implies QB has extension records only for Mercer.
✗Run #2The email to grace.yamamoto only identifies LN-2026-00184 as affected. It explicitly states 'All other active pipeline loans (LN-2026 series) — reviewed clean' in the rate-watch post, incorrectly concluding no other loans had Stripe extension charges without QB records.
✓Run #3The email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com identifies that 13 active-pipeline loans have Stripe extension charges with no QuickBooks records, while Mercer (LN-2026-00184) is the only loan with QB extension bill records. This is consistent with the criterion's description.
✗Run #4The email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com describes a systemic gap (~$20,700 across 50 charges/33+ loans) but does not specifically identify that the active-pipeline loans beyond Mercer have zero QuickBooks records. The agent did not differentiate active-pipeline from historical loans in the reconciliation.
✗Run #5The Grace email states 0 QB bills for all 50 Stripe charges. The criteria require identifying Mercer (LN-2026-00184) as the one exception with QB records, but the agent found no QB records for any loan at all, and LN-2026-00184 is not in the agent's loan set.
✗Run #6The agent did not identify the active-pipeline loans (Rivera, Esposito, Pham, Lombardi, Flores, Freeman, Reyes/LN-2026-00558) that have Stripe charges with no QB records. The Grace email is framed around 6 different loans and does not identify these active-pipeline loans as having zero QB extension records.
47 The loan activity note on LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) or the email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com mentions that the rate_lock_expiration on the Mercer loan (2026-03-23) has not been updated despite multiple extension charges being processed.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✓ 17%

The email notes 'Lock expired 3/23/2026' as a data point but does not flag the stale field as a finding.
✗Run #3Neither the loan activity note on LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) nor the email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com mentions a stale or outdated rate_lock_expiration date. The Mercer note focuses only on QB vs. Stripe reconciliation.
✗Run #4Neither the activity note on LN-2026-00184 nor the email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com mentions that the rate_lock_expiration (2026-03-23) has not been updated despite multiple extension charges being processed.
✗Run #5The agent never worked with LN-2026-00184 (Mercer). Neither the Grace email nor any LOS activity note in the trajectory references the Mercer loan's stale rate_lock_expiration date of 2026-03-23.
✓Run #6The agent retrieved the Mercer rate lock status (item 38–39) and found it expired 2026-03-23, 19 days past. The Mercer compliance_alert activity note mentions 'lock expired,' and this finding is consistent with being referenced in the Grace email as well.
48 The #rate-watch message (via conversations_add_message to C005) includes that 8 active-pipeline loans have unreconciled Stripe extension charges (or similar count of affected loans).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The #rate-watch message reports 13 active (2025-vintage) loans, not 8. The expected count per the criteria is approximately 8 active-pipeline loans. The figure 8 does not appear in the message.
✗Run #2The #rate-watch message states 'All other active pipeline loans reviewed — no additional extension charges found in Stripe for LN-2026-series loans outside this file.' The message implies only 1 affected loan, not 8. No count of affected loans is provided.
✗Run #3The #rate-watch message references 14 total loans (13 Stripe-charged + 1 Mercer reverse gap), not 8 active-pipeline loans with unreconciled Stripe charges as the criteria expect.
✗Run #4The #rate-watch message describes 4 affected loans specifically (LN-2026-00184, LN-2026-00613, LN-2026-00622, LN-2026-00617) and mentions 33+ loans in the historical gap, but does not include a count of ~8 active-pipeline loans with unreconciled Stripe charges.
✗Run #5The #rate-watch post covers 13 active 2025-series loans, not 8 active-pipeline 2026 loans. The count in the message would be 13 (or 42 total), not 8. The agent's loan dataset does not align with the criteria's expected active-pipeline set.
✗Run #6The #rate-watch message covers 6 flagged loans, not 8 active-pipeline loans. The agent's audit identified a different set of loans than the actual data, so the count of affected loans in the message does not match the ~8 required.
49 The #rate-watch message (via conversations_add_message to C005) includes a total dollar exposure of approximately $3,650 to $4,900 for unreconciled Stripe extension charges across active-pipeline loans (or similar quantitative audit summary).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The #rate-watch message reports a total unreconciled exposure of $24,725 (or $6,400 for active pipeline). The expected range per the criteria is $3,650–$4,900. Neither figure falls in this range.
✗Run #2The #rate-watch message shows 'Unreconciled exposure: $500.00', which is far below the expected range of $3,650–$4,900. The agent missed seven additional affected loans with Stripe charges and no QuickBooks records.
✗Run #3The #rate-watch message states a total exposure of $7,550, which is outside the expected range of $3,650–$4,900. The agent's dataset produced different totals than the criteria expect.
✗Run #4The #rate-watch message mentions $750 uncollected for the Mercer loan and ~$20,700 systemic historical gap, but does not include a total dollar exposure in the range of $3,650–$4,900 for active-pipeline loans with unreconciled Stripe charges.
✗Run #5The #rate-watch post includes $24,325 as the total (all 50 charges across all 42 loans), not a figure in the $3,650-$4,900 range representing only active-pipeline unreconciled charges on the 2026-series loans.
✗Run #6The #rate-watch message uses the agent's total of $24,425 unreconciled gap, which is far outside the $3,650–$4,900 range for active-pipeline-only unreconciled charges. The agent computed the exposure across all loans including non-active ones rather than isolating active-pipeline loans.
